Harris County.filingNotes: Harris County is one of only a handful of Texas counties large enough to have dedicated statutory probate courts — Probate Courts No. 1, 2, 3, and 4 — each presided over by a full-time probate judge. Cases are randomly assigned among the four courts at filing.
Harris County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested independent administrations typically conclude in 4–8 months in Harris County, depending on the docket of the assigned probate court.

Fort Bend County.courtName: Fort Bend County Court at Law No. 2
Fort Bend County.courtType: Statutory county court at law (probate jurisdiction)
Fort Bend County.filingNotes: Probate matters in Fort Bend County are heard by County Court at Law No. 2 in the Justice Center in Richmond. Filings are made through the Fort Bend County Clerk.
Fort Bend County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Fort Bend County typically take 3–6 months from filing to closing of administration.

Galveston County.filingNotes: Galveston County has a dedicated probate court at the Galveston County Courthouse. The court hears probate, guardianship, and mental health matters for the entire county, including League City, Friendswood, Texas City, and the Bay Area.
Galveston County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Galveston County typically take 3–6 months.

Brazoria County.courtName: Brazoria County Court at Law No. 4 and Probate Court
Brazoria County.courtType: Statutory county court at law (probate jurisdiction)
Brazoria County.courtAddress: 111 East Locust Street, Room 201, Angleton, TX 77515
Brazoria County.filingNotes: Brazoria County probate matters are heard by County Court at Law No. 4 at the courthouse in Angleton. Filings are made through the Brazoria County Clerk.
Brazoria County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Brazoria County typically take 3–6 months.

Montgomery County.filingNotes: Montgomery County has a dedicated statutory probate court — Probate Court No. 1 — that hears all probate, guardianship, and mental health matters for the county. The court is located at the Lee G. Alworth Building in downtown Conroe.
Montgomery County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Montgomery County typically take 4–6 months.

Waller County.filingNotes: Waller County is a smaller rural county where probate matters are heard by the constitutional county court. Filings are made through the Waller County Clerk in Hempstead.
Waller County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Waller County typically take 3–5 months — dockets in smaller counties often move faster than in metropolitan Harris.

Chambers County.filingNotes: Chambers County probate matters are heard by the constitutional county court at the courthouse in Anahuac. The county also operates a branch annex in Mont Belvieu at 10616 Eagle Drive for many county clerk services.
Chambers County.estimatedTimeline: Uncontested probate matters in Chambers County typically take 3–5 months.

Harris / Fort Bend / Waller County.filingNotes: Some communities — most notably Katy — sit at the intersection of three counties. The county where probate is filed is determined by the deceased's legal residence at the time of death, not by ZIP code or mailing address. Our firm regularly files in all three jurisdictions.
Harris / Fort Bend / Waller County.estimatedTimeline: Timelines vary by the county where probate is opened.
